resources.string: Local port %1$d is already in use. Stop the other app using it or select another port.

Many connections are arriving with incorrect proxy settings. This usually means Telegram still has old entries for this local proxy. Delete them, then add the current link again. This stops unnecessary reconnects, reduces battery use, and helps Telegram connect without delays.
Settings are saved automatically. Connection changes apply when you leave this screen; a running proxy will restart.
Use 127.0.0.1 for this phone only. Use 0.0.0.0 only for access from your local network.
After changing the secret, add the proxy to Telegram again.
Telegram connections using the current proxy link will stop working. You will need to add the proxy to Telegram again.
Advanced setting. Each line assigns a direct WebSocket target to a Telegram datacenter. Remove a line to use an enabled fallback route.
Chooses routes and safe connection defaults. Turn this off to configure the options below manually.
Adds an alternate route when direct connections are blocked or unreliable.
WebSocket → Cloudflare → TCP. Recommended for most networks.
WebSocket → TCP → Cloudflare. Prefers direct routes when possible.
Cloudflare → WebSocket → TCP. Try this if Telegram media does not load.
After WebSocket fails, try Cloudflare before TCP
When off, TCP is tried before Cloudflare.
Use only a custom Cloudflare domain
Replaces the shared domain list with this domain. Use it only when shared domains are consistently unavailable.
Tests available domains against Telegram datacenters and selects the fastest working one for the current proxy run.
Advanced settings. Defaults suit most devices; change one value at a time when troubleshooting.
Choose the balance between lower battery use and reliable background connections.
Prevents deep sleep for the most reliable screen-off operation. Turning it off reduces idle power use.
Keeps direct connections ready for faster startup. When off, no prepared connections are used; the saved manual pool size is preserved.
Longer intervals reduce radio wakeups but can make idle connections less reliable.
Check routes after startup and network changes
Tests direct WebSocket, Cloudflare, and TCP to detect working routes.
Updates the shared domain list in the background. Built-in domains remain available when disabled.
Show traffic in the service notification
Shows and refreshes the traffic counter every 5 seconds. When disabled, traffic is not shown and no periodic notification updates run.
Use the device Material You palette
View live connection events and create a diagnostic report.
Show detailed statistics on the main screen
Displays connection, route, pool, and error counters.
Use diagnostics when Telegram cannot connect, media does not load, or the proxy is unstable. Start diagnostics, reproduce the problem in Telegram, then return here and finish.
Return to Telegram and reproduce the problem. When finished, come back here, finish diagnostics, and share the report from the top bar.
The report keeps the latest 4 MB of technical connection events. Message and media contents and the proxy secret are not recorded.
Enter a valid IP address or hostname
Use DC:IP with DC 1–5 or 203, one per line
Buffer must be between 4 and 4096 KB
Pool size must be between 0 and 16
Shows route recommendations when automatic connection mode is off
CF proxy is disabled or no domains are available
Notification permission is required for the foreground service
Unable to open the release page
Unable to download or verify the update
Unable to open the verified update installer
Allow TG WS Proxy to install unknown apps, then tap Update again
Unable to open the install permission settings
Direct connections repeatedly time out on this network. Cloudflare is recommended.
Direct connections repeatedly time out while Cloudflare is stable. Cloudflare first is recommended.
Cloudflare repeatedly fails while direct connections are stable. Direct connection first is recommended.
This guide describes how the proxy works, what each setting changes, and how to diagnose connection problems.

Telegram connects to a local MTProto proxy on this device. The app reads the requested Telegram datacenter and forwards the encrypted connection through direct WebSocket, Cloudflare, or direct TCP. It performs lightweight route checks after startup and network changes. The active route order is shown on the main screen. Traffic contents are not decrypted by the interface or written to the connection log.

1. Start the proxy on the main screen.
2. Tap “Open in Telegram.”
3. Confirm the proxy in Telegram.
4. Keep the service notification enabled.

Settings are saved automatically. A running proxy restarts after leaving settings only when connection settings actually changed.

“Configure connection automatically” starts each proxy run and each new network with WebSocket → Cloudflare → TCP and safe connection defaults. If it temporarily selects Cloudflare → WebSocket → TCP, prepared direct WebSocket connections are disabled. If only one datacenter’s media route stops returning data, the app temporarily sends only that media route through Cloudflare. The active route order is shown on the main screen.\n\nTurn automatic configuration off to reveal manual routing options, Direct WebSocket targets, and Connection tuning directly below the switch. Manual values remain saved while automatic configuration is enabled. Settings are saved automatically; connection changes apply when you leave Settings and the running proxy restarts.

These controls are available when automatic connection mode is off. “Use Cloudflare fallback” adds the alternate route. Route order selects WebSocket or Cloudflare first; the additional switch controls whether Cloudflare or TCP follows a failed WebSocket attempt.\n\n“Use only a custom Cloudflare domain” replaces the entire shared list. Use it only when shared domains are consistently unavailable. “Test and select a domain” checks available domains against Telegram datacenters. HTTP 429 means a shared domain reached its connection limit; the app temporarily skips it.

These controls are available when “Automatic connection mode” is off. A DC:IP line enables direct WebSocket for one Telegram datacenter. Telegram selects the datacenter automatically; the app only selects its route. Removing a line makes that datacenter use Cloudflare or TCP instead. Leave the default list unchanged unless you are isolating a routing problem or following a support instruction.

These controls are available when “Automatic connection mode” is off. The pool keeps direct WebSocket connections ready to reduce startup delay. The buffer controls socket memory used during transfers. The default pool size and 256 KB buffer are suitable for most devices. Lower the pool only to reduce memory and connection usage. Do not increase these values without a measured reason.

The Power usage section lets you balance lower battery use against background connection reliability. It contains independent controls for device sleep, prepared connections, keepalive packets, route checks, and shared Cloudflare-domain updates. “Keep device awake” gives the most reliable screen-off operation but prevents deep sleep. Disable it first when investigating high idle power use. If connections then stop with the screen off, enable it again or allow unrestricted background operation in Android settings. “Prepare WebSocket connections” reduces connection delay but retains idle sockets. Route checks and domain updates can be disabled independently; automatic routing can still react to failures from real Telegram sessions.\n\nThe WebSocket keepalive interval applies to idle active sessions. 30 seconds prioritizes reliability; 90 or 180 seconds reduces cellular radio wakeups, but some networks may close idle connections.

Choose System, Light, or Dark appearance. On Android 12 and newer, adaptive colors use the Material You palette generated from the device wallpaper. Appearance changes are applied and saved automatically.

The screen shows the latest 300 connection events from memory. To prepare a report, open Connection diagnostics, tap “Start diagnostics,” reproduce the problem in Telegram, return here, and tap “Finish diagnostics.” Then use “Share report” in the top bar.\n\nDiagnostics continues until manually finished and keeps approximately 4 MB of the latest technical events, discarding the oldest first. Message and media contents and the proxy secret are not recorded. Exported reports also redact proxy links and IP addresses. Saved files expire after 7 days. Deleting a saved report does not clear the events shown on screen.

Media does not load in automatic mode: keep the download open while the app detects the stalled media connection and retries that datacenter through Cloudflare.

Media does not load in manual mode: select “Try Cloudflare first” and leave Settings to apply the change.

Cloudflare fails: turn automatic mode off, test the domains, and look for HTTP 429 in the connection log.

Connections stop with the screen off: allow background operation and disable battery optimization for the app.

Problem persists: start Connection diagnostics, reproduce the issue, finish diagnostics, and share the report.

Keep the foreground-service notification enabled. Some Android vendors may still restrict background networking; exclude the app from battery optimization if connections stop when the screen is off. If “Keep device awake” is off, enable it again before investigating other screen-off failures.
An on-device MTProto proxy for Telegram with WebSocket transport and automatic Cloudflare/TCP fallback.
Runs entirely on your device without root, an account, ads, or analytics.
Uses pooled TLS WebSocket connections with Cloudflare and direct TCP fallback.
Includes a foreground service, notification controls, Quick Settings tile, and live statistics.
Inspired by tg-ws-proxy by Flowseal. This Android implementation is developed independently.
